paper_title: Pollination and Stigma Receptivity Studies in Cassava

methods.description: This method describes the controlled pollination process used to prevent undesirable pollinations and ensure proper pollen transfer in cassava breeding experiments.

steps.description: Early in the morning, experienced field workers identified inflorescences bearing flowers ready for anthesis and covered them with a mesh bag to prevent undesirable pollinations.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and cover inflorescences with mesh bags to prevent unwanted pollination

steps.description: Male flowers were collected early in the morning before their anthesis and placed in plastic bottles with their origin properly identified.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect and store male flowers in labeled plastic bottles

steps.description: Pollination was done by rubbing anthers on stigmas until it was visible that they were covered with pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform pollination by rubbing anthers on stigmas

steps.description: The isolation bag, removed for the pollination procedure, was immediately placed back and kept protecting the inflorescence until the material was collected for analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: Replace isolation bag after pollination to protect inflorescence

methods.method_name: Fixation and Staining Protocol for Microscopy Study
methods.description: This method outlines the process for fixing and staining cassava flowers to study pollen tube growth using microscopy.

steps.description: Upon collection, the flowering stem was cut about 0.5 cm below bracts. The latter were removed and flowers were immediately submerged in the fixation solution (ethanol: acetic acid, 3:1 v/v).
steps.searchable_summary: Submerge flowers in fixation solution after collection

steps.description: Preparation of samples for pollen tube growth analysis began with softening and clearing of tissue by submerging samples in 8 N NaOH.
steps.searchable_summary: Soften and clear tissue by submerging in NaOH

steps.description: Two 1-h washes with a 0.5 N K2HPO4 buffer solution of pH 10-12 were followed by sample incubation in decolorized 0.1% aniline blue solution in K3PO4.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash samples with buffer solution and incubate in aniline blue

steps.description: The number of pollen grains on stigma visible after processing and the rate of pollen tube growth through the pistil were assessed using a fluorescence microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess pollen grains and tube growth using fluorescence microscopy

methods.method_name: Experimental Design for Stigma Receptivity Assessment
methods.description: This method describes the experimental design used to assess stigma receptivity by fruit and seed setting in cassava.

steps.description: In the first trial, similar number of female cyathia were randomly assigned to one of the following treatments: a) no coverage of cyathia, open pollination; b) the coverage of cyathia as described in 2.3 but mesh bags were removed one day after anthesis (1 DAA); c) the coverage was removed 2 DAA and d) mesh bags were removed 3 DAA.
steps.searchable_summary: Assign female cyathia to different pollination treatments

steps.description: The fruit set for each treatment was established 60 DAA.
steps.searchable_summary: Establish fruit set for each treatment 60 days after anthesis

steps.description: In the second trial, fruits were covered again with mesh bags as they approached maturity, so seeds could be collected and counted upon dehiscence.
steps.searchable_summary: Cover fruits with mesh bags and collect seeds upon dehiscence

methods.method_name: Microscope Study of Stigma Receptivity and Pollen Tube Growth
methods.description: This method investigates the period of stigma receptivity and the effect of pollination timing on pollen tube growth in cassava.

steps.description: Seventy female flowers from clone SM 1219-9 were isolated from unwanted pollination 1-2 days before anthesis.
steps.searchable_summary: Isolate female flowers from unwanted pollination

steps.description: The remaining 60 flowers were either self-pollinated or crossed with pollen from genotype HMC1.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform self-pollination or cross-pollination with HMC1 pollen

steps.description: Samples were collected and fixed 24, 48 or 72 h after pollination and stained with aniline blue.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect and fix samples at different times post-pollination

steps.description: Two response variables were considered: score on the number of pollen grains on the stigma surface and the furthermost position of the longest pollen tube tip.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze pollen grain count and pollen tube tip position
